title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited by their inability to maintain context across interactions and see the full codebase, comparing them to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He advocates for architectural improvements to enable greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx contends that current coding agents are limited by two failures: they cannot retain context across interactions and cannot see the full codebase, a gap he likens to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He argues the fix is architectural rather than a matter of larger context windows alone, with attention to how monorepo structure can surface the relevant parts of a codebase to an agent. The takeaway is that improving how context is maintained and delivered is the path to greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.
